description_long: This is a robust hammer featuring a solid, rectangular metal head with a flat striking face and a slightly chamfered edge. The head is dark, likely made of steel, and is securely mounted on a wooden handle. The handle is straight, with a natural wood grain visible and an orange-tinted finish at the lower end, possibly for improved grip or visibility. The hammer shows a utilitarian design, suitable for heavy-duty tasks such as driving nails or breaking objects. The overall construction suggests durability and reliability, making it ideal for use in urban maintenance, construction, or repair scenarios. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its functional purpose.
description: A sturdy hammer with a dark metal head and a straight wooden handle featuring an orange-tinted grip. Designed for heavy-duty urban tasks, it has a practical, no-frills appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat striking face of the dark metal head and the orange-tinted end of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the straight wooden handle and the rectangular profile of the metal head.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the metal head and the top of the handle, with the orange grip visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle's curvature and the head's thickness, with the orange grip extending along the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a natural grain and an orange-tinted painted lower section for grip enhancement.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular steel head with a flat striking face, typical of a sledge or engineer's hammer, no claws or peen features.
